blocks.text: DTC P0344: VTC Cam Sensor Noise

The TDC sensor consists of a pulsar rotor and semiconductors that detect rotor position and intake camshaft timing. When the engine starts, the pulsar rotor rotates, causing the magnetic flux of the TDC sensor to fluctuate. The TDC sensor also detects the top dead center of each cylinder, and the PGM-FI ECU uses the pulse signals from the TDC sensor and the crank sensor to determine the intake camshaft position. If the abnormal pulse signal from the TDC sensor is input more than a predetermined number of times, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: If the number of pulse signals from the TDC sensor sent during the pulse signal interval from the crank sensor is more than 30 states other than 6.

items.text: Noise in code between PGM-FI ECU and TDC sensor (TDCAM line signal)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
